# Bounded summary lease-hold ‚ÄĒ cap sessions-per-sweep so the picker can interleave

## Context

Split from `#13586` (the heavy-maintenance fair picker). The picker (Part 1) landed in its PR and re-prioritizes the heavy lease at every release boundary ‚ÄĒ so a weeks-stale `golden-path` / starved `dream` now out-ranks a re-firing `summary`. This ticket is the **separable Part 2**: bounding how long `summary` holds the lease per sweep, so the picker gets release boundaries *frequently* rather than only after a whole drift batch.

It is filed separately because it touches a different subsystem (Memory Core `SessionService` + the **MCP-server config template**, ADR 0019 + `mcp-config-template-change-guide`), not the orchestrator scheduling layer `#13586` lives in.

## The Problem

`SessionService.summarizeSessions` drains the *entire* drift list in one child run (`SessionService.mjs:1386`, `for (let i = 0; i < total; i += batchSize)` over `sessionsToSummarize` = the full `findSessionsToSummarize()` result). While that child holds the heavy-maintenance lease, every other heavy task ‚ÄĒ `dream`, `backup`, `memory-summary-backfill` ‚ÄĒ is deferred. The live forensic (`get_rem_pipeline_state`: 371 undigested, `recentCycles: []`) shows `dream` starved behind exactly this hold.

Part 1's staleness picker only chooses a *new* winner when the lease is **free** (post-release). So the shorter each `summary` hold, the sooner `dream`/`golden-path` get their turn. With an unbounded hold, the picker waits out the whole batch.

## The Architectural Reality

- Drain loop: `ai/services/memory-core/SessionService.mjs:1379-1387` ‚ÄĒ `total = sessionsToSummarize.length`; the loop processes all of them before the child exits + releases the lease.
- Config precedent: `summarizationBatchLimit` is a leaf at `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs:123` (`leaf(2000)`) + mirrored in `config.mjs`, read at the use site as `aiConfig.summarizationBatchLimit` (ADR 0019 reactive Provider SSOT). A new `maxSessionsPerSummarySweep` leaf follows the same pattern.
- The drift sweep is self-continuing: processed sessions self-heal (count matches, `#13579`), so the next sweep re-derives the *remaining* drift ‚ÄĒ capping per-sweep does not drop work, it chunks it.

## The Fix

- Add a config-driven `maxSessionsPerSummarySweep` leaf (small default, e.g. `5`) in `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs` + `config.mjs`, per the mcp-config-template-change-guide.
- Extract a pure `capSessionsForSweep(sessions, maxPerSweep)` helper (slice to N when N is a positive integer, else passthrough); apply it to `sessionsToSummarize` before the loop. The child exits + releases the lease after ‚Č§ N sessions; the next sweep continues the backlog.
- Read `aiConfig.maxSessionsPerSummarySweep` at the use site (never re-implement/alias ‚ÄĒ ADR 0019).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `capSessionsForSweep` is a pure, unit-tested helper: caps to a positive-integer N, passthrough otherwise.
- [ ] `SessionService.summarizeSessions` processes ‚Č§ `maxSessionsPerSummarySweep` sessions per child run, then exits + releases the lease; remaining drift persists for the next sweep. Unit-tested at the helper grain.
- [ ] `maxSessionsPerSummarySweep` is a config leaf (template + config.mjs), env-overridable, read at the use site per ADR 0019.
- [ ] **Post-merge (flagged):** on the live orchestrator, `summary` no longer holds the lease for a whole batch; the deferral log shows `dream`/`backup` interleaving within one cap-window; `undigested` (REM) drains.

## Out of Scope

- The fair picker itself ‚ÄĒ `#13586` (delivered).
- The summary backlog never reaching idle (dead-letter + metric reconcile) ‚ÄĒ `#13590`.
- The optimal cap value / per-session throughput tuning ‚ÄĒ an empirical follow-up once the mechanism lands.

## Related

- `#13586` ‚ÄĒ heavy-maintenance fair picker (Part 1; this is Part 2).
- `#13590` ‚ÄĒ drift summarization never dead-letters / settles.
- `#12065` ‚ÄĒ Orchestrator-as-SSOT for the REM pipeline.

## Decision Record impact

`none` ‚ÄĒ a config-driven bound within the existing summarization drain; ADR 0019-aligned (new leaf, use-site read).

Release classification: post-v13 agent-OS stability hardening. Boardless; board #13 (Agent Harness) candidate.

Retrieval Hint: "bounded summary lease-hold maxSessionsPerSummarySweep capSessionsForSweep" / `SessionService.mjs:1386` drain loop / split from #13586 Part 2

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-20T05:50:57Z

## Implementation plan ‚ÄĒ grounded (ADR 0019 + ADR 0014 cloud-safe). Grace taking this.

Read ADR 0019 (the config-SSOT read-gate). The bounded-hold lands as:

1. **Config leaf (declarative ‚ÄĒ ADR 0019 ¬ß5.2):** add `maxSessionsPerSummarySweep: leaf(<N>, 'NEO_MC_MAX_SESSIONS_PER_SUMMARY_SWEEP', 'number')` to `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs` (sibling of `summarizationBatchLimit:123`). Verify the `config.mjs` overlay pattern at impl (thin child of deltas ‚ÄĒ only add there if the repo's existing leaves mirror). Default small (‚Čą5) so the hold is bounded to a few sessions/sweep; the cap-vs-model-warmup tradeoff is the OQ4 tuning follow-up.

2. **Pure cap helper (B4-safe, unit-testable):** extract `capSessionsForSweep(sessions, maxPerSweep)` ‚ÄĒ `slice(0, N)` for a positive integer N, else passthrough. Unit-tested **without** mutating the `aiConfig` singleton (ADR 0019 ¬ß4 B4 ‚ÄĒ the live-DB-bleed class).

3. **Use-site read (no alias / export / thread ‚ÄĒ ADR 0019 ¬ß5.1):** `SessionService.summarizeSessions` reads `aiConfig.maxSessionsPerSummarySweep` directly and applies `capSessionsForSweep` to the drift list before the loop (`SessionService.mjs:1386`). The child exits + releases the heavy lease after ‚Č§ N sessions; the next sweep re-derives the remainder.

4. **Cloud-safe (ADR 0014):** the cap applies uniformly wherever `summary` runs. The cloud profile runs `summary` (per the cloud-safety V-B-A on #13594), so it's beneficial there too ‚ÄĒ shorter holds ‚Üí `dream`/`golden-path` interleave sooner. No deployment-conditional logic.

5. **Close-contract:** `Resolves #13592`; cross-family review (Vega/GPT).

Self-assigned. Grounding now; implementing on fresh capacity ‚ÄĒ an SSOT + live-drain change earns un-fatigued focus (ADR 0019's own lesson: diligence-while-deep is empirically insufficient for this surface).

## Contract Ledger + AC4 residual annotation (per @neo-gpt's #13666 review)

**Contract Ledger ‚ÄĒ `maxSessionsPerSummarySweep`:**

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `maxSessionsPerSummarySweep` config leaf | ADR-0019 reactive Provider SSOT + the `summarizationBatchLimit` sibling precedent | Small-default (5), env-overridable (`NEO_MC_MAX_SESSIONS_PER_SUMMARY_SWEEP`) integer leaf, read at the use site; `SessionService.summarizeSessions` caps the drift list to ‚Č§ N per child run before the drain loop | Non-positive / unset = no bound (passthrough) ‚ÄĒ preserves the prior unbounded behavior | Leaf JSDoc + the `capSessionsForSweep` module JSDoc name the lease-release / interleave rationale | `config.template.spec` (default + env override) + `capSessionsForSweep.spec` (cap / passthrough / no-mutation) |

**AC4 residual annotation:** AC4 (the live orchestrator shows `dream`/`backup` interleaving within a cap-window + `undigested` drains) is **`[L3-deferred ‚ÄĒ operator handoff needed]`** ‚ÄĒ observable only on the running orchestrator, not reachable in CI. Carried as the Post-Merge Validation item in PR #13666.
